body.probe: Newly inserted orphan node (no parent_id) surfaces in memory_search within 1 turn without restart
body.title: cortex.search orphan supplement threshold — 80-node gate never opens at scale
body.result: Added unconditional orphan rescue pass in cortex.search(): always fetches up to 20 recently-activated nodes WHERE parent_id IS NULL and adds them to all_memories before scoring, regardless of traversal pool size. Deduped against traversal+supplement. The old 80-node supplement gate was never opening at 11k+ memorie scale. 3 unit tests verify SQL always fires, nodes enter candidate pool, and no dedup violations. 146 pass.
body.description: The orphan supplement (activation-index fallback) in cortex.search() only runs when traversal pool < 80 nodes. At 11k+ memories rooted at CP roots, this gate never opens. Any node with parent_id=NULL is invisible to search. Fix options: (a) always add orphan nodes with activation_count > N to supplement pool regardless of traversal size, (b) enforce parent assignment on insert (store_memory raises if parent_id is None). Igor flagged this as design debt during 2026-03-23c teaching session.
